name: Yemeni Civil War (Saudi-led intervention, blockade, famine)
summary: The Yemeni Civil War began when Ansar Allah (the Houthis) seized Sana'a in 2014 and escalated after the Saudi-led coalition intervened in March 2015 on behalf of the internationally recognized government. The most cited comprehensive mortality model, by UNDP/Pardee, estimated 377,000 deaths by the end of 2021, most of them indirect deaths from hunger, disease, water, sanitation, and health-system collapse. Direct civilian harm is best documented for Saudi-led coalition airstrikes and Houthi/Saleh shelling, mines, and missile attacks, but combatant deaths by side and indirect deaths by perpetrator remain poorly quantified.

sides.name: Ansar Allah / Houthi-led Sana'a authorities and allied Saleh forces
members: Forces loyal to Ali Abdullah Saleh until 2017

military_killed.notes: The low is only a documented subset: the UN Panel of Experts reportedly listed 1,406 Houthi-recruited children killed in 2020 and 562 in January-May 2021. No credible total Houthi/Saleh combatant death toll for 2014-2026 was found.

breakdown.notes: ACLED reported over 1,900 civilian fatalities from direct civilian targeting by Houthis and allies in its 2015-mid-2019 data.
UN figures cited by HRW attributed 475 civilian deaths to Houthi-aligned shelling between 1 July 2015 and 30 June 2016; HRW separately documented 7 indiscriminate attacks killing 139 civilians.

breakdown.victims_group: Civilians in Hodeida, Taizz and other contaminated areas
breakdown.notes: HRW cited CIMP data that landmines killed or injured at least 140 civilians in Hodeida and Taizz since January 2018, but warned the data underreported the real toll.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: No authoritative cumulative 2014-2026 total was found. The 1,900 figure is a partial ACLED count through mid-2019 for direct targeting only and excludes many later or differently coded civilian deaths.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: Yemeni civilians in Houthi-controlled and contested areas
deaths_from_actions.notes: Mwatana/GRC concluded that Ansar Allah, along with the Saudi/UAE-led coalition, used starvation as a method of warfare through aid obstruction, arrests/intimidation of aid workers, blocking convoys, seizure of aid property, shelling areas affecting food access, and mining the Red Sea Mills. The share of UNDP's 223,000-227,000 indirect deaths attributable to Ansar Allah cannot be quantified from available sources.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: Civilians displaced or prevented from returning by mines and siege conditions
deaths_from_actions.notes: Houthi-laid landmines impeded return, farming, water access, and aid delivery; no credible estimate converts this to indirect deaths.

sides.name: Internationally recognized Yemeni government and Saudi-led coalition
members: Republic of Yemen Government / internationally recognized government

military_killed.notes: No consolidated 2014-2026 combatant death toll by coalition/government side was found. Known fragments include 45 UAE soldiers killed in September 2015 and a Marib attack reported to have killed 52 Emirati, 10 Saudi, 5 Bahraini, and 36 pro-Hadi Yemeni troops.

breakdown.notes: AOAV recorded 4,946 civilian fatalities attributed to the Saudi-led coalition from explosive weapons in 2014-2023.

ACLED's Saudi-led coalition actor profile states that over 9,200 civilians were estimated killed as a result of coalition operations from March 2015 to July 2023, likely an undercount because it excludes some deaths at military sites.
ACLED's 2015-mid-2019 release reported that the Saudi-led coalition and allies were responsible for over 8,000 of about 11,700 direct civilian-targeting fatalities.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: The low and high are not strict statistical confidence bounds; they are two conservative event-data measurements with different methodologies. The true direct civilian toll from coalition operations may be higher.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: Yemeni civilians dependent on imports, fuel, medical travel, water and health systems
deaths_from_actions.notes: HRW reported that Saudi-led coalition import restrictions and naval/air blockade measures worsened the humanitarian crisis and restricted fuel, food, medical access, and water/hospital systems. The coalition likely contributed substantially to UNDP's 223,000-227,000 indirect deaths by end-2021, but no source found quantifies the coalition-only share.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: Yemeni civilians affected by attacks on food, water, health, transport and electricity infrastructure
deaths_from_actions.notes: Mwatana/GRC concluded that the Saudi/UAE-led coalition attacked objects indispensable to survival, including farms, water facilities, fishing boats, markets, food and water storage and production sites, roads, bridges, electricity, and health facilities, supporting a finding of starvation as a method of warfare. No credible numerical perpetrator-specific indirect-death estimate was found.

sides.name: UAE-backed Southern Transitional Council and other southern / anti-Houthi armed groups

military_killed.notes: No consolidated public combatant death toll by faction was found.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: Credible reporting attributes detention abuses, torture and deaths in unofficial prisons to UAE-backed, government-backed and Houthi forces, but available figures are not cleanly separated for STC/UAE-backed forces.

sides.name: AQAP, Islamic State in Yemen, and other jihadist actors

military_killed.notes: No consolidated 2014-2026 death toll for AQAP/IS-Yemen fighters was found.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: AQAP and IS-Yemen carried out attacks, but no authoritative cumulative civilian-death estimate for the war period was found.

sides.name: United States, United Kingdom, and Israel operations against Houthi-controlled Yemen

military_killed.notes: No meaningful own-combatant death toll for these external forces in Yemen was found.

breakdown.notes: HRW, citing Yemen Data Project, reported at least 238 civilians killed and 467 injured by U.S. airstrikes between 15 March and 6 May 2025.
HRW reported that a U.S. strike on Ras Issa Port on 17 April 2025 killed 84 civilians and injured more than 150.
HRW reported that a U.S. strike on a migrant detention center in Saada on 28 April 2025 killed over 68 civilians; Amnesty called the strike indiscriminate.
HRW, citing CPJ, reported that an Israeli strike on a Sana'a media complex on 10 September 2025 killed 31 journalists.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: These deaths are part of the later regionalized Yemen/Red Sea escalation and should not be merged uncritically with earlier Saudi-led coalition totals.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: Yemeni civilians dependent on airports, ports, fuel, electricity and medical travel
deaths_from_actions.notes: U.S. and Israeli strikes damaged ports, airports and infrastructure, but no credible source was found translating this into indirect-death estimates.

totals.notes: UNDP/Pardee estimated 377,000 total direct and indirect war deaths by the end of 2021, including about 154,000 direct and 223,000 indirect deaths. No updated comprehensive all-cause model through 2026 was found, so the current total is higher but not responsibly quantifiable from the sources consulted.

key_atrocities.name: Mokha residential compound airstrike
key_atrocities.notes: HRW reported at least 57 civilian deaths; other reports put the toll between 65 and 120.

key_atrocities.name: Sanaa funeral hall airstrike
key_atrocities.notes: UN officials reported more than 140 killed and more than 525 wounded; some later summaries cite 155 killed.

key_atrocities.name: Dahyan / Saada school-bus airstrike
key_atrocities.victims_group: Children and civilians in market / school bus
key_atrocities.notes: ICRC/Xinhua reported 51 killed, including 40 children; HRW documented 34 named deaths and called it an apparent war crime.

key_atrocities.name: Dhamar detention-facility airstrike
key_atrocities.notes: ICRC said every detainee was killed or injured; reports citing ICRC put at least 130 prisoners presumed dead.

key_atrocities.name: Houthi/Saleh shelling of Aden and Taizz
key_atrocities.notes: HRW documented seven indiscriminate attacks killing 139 civilians; UN figures cited by HRW attributed 475 civilian deaths to Houthi-aligned shelling from July 2015 to June 2016.

key_atrocities.name: Taizz artillery attacks
key_atrocities.notes: HRW reported at least 30 civilians killed and over 160 wounded in ten days of shelling in May 2017.

key_atrocities.name: Marib fuel-station missile/drone attack
key_atrocities.notes: Yemeni government reports cited by Al Jazeera said at least 17 people were killed; Houthis denied targeting civilians.

key_atrocities.name: Ras Issa Port strike
key_atrocities.notes: HRW reported 84 civilians killed and more than 150 injured.

key_atrocities.name: Saada migrant detention-center strike
key_atrocities.victims_group: African migrants detained by Houthi authorities
key_atrocities.notes: HRW reported over 68 civilians killed; Amnesty concluded the strike was indiscriminate and should be investigated as a war crime.

key_atrocities.name: Sana'a media-complex strike
key_atrocities.notes: HRW, citing the Committee to Protect Journalists, reported 31 journalists killed.

uncertainty_notes: The best comprehensive all-cause toll remains the UNDP/Pardee estimate of 377,000 deaths by end-2021. Direct event data and civilian-impact monitoring continue after 2021, but there is no updated all-cause 2026 model. Military deaths by side and indirect deaths by perpetrator are especially uncertain; the indirect deaths are shared products of blockade, siege, infrastructure destruction, aid obstruction, economic collapse, disease, hunger, displacement and health-system breakdown.
